156.reason: [Error: When encountering a single '&' or '|', the code sets ErrorLine but does not skip the character properly. It should also skip the character to avoid infinite loop or incorrect parsing. Additionally, the error should be recorded and the program should continue, but the current logic does not handle the case where the error occurs at the end of the file or when pos+1 is out of bounds.]

157.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer records the error line but does not stop processing or skip the rest of the input properly. More critically, it fails to check if pos+1 is within bounds, which can cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' appears at the end of file. Additionally, after detecting the error, it only increments pos by 1 and continues, potentially causing further misparsing or missing the actual error location if newline hasn't been accounted for. However, the primary issue relevant to the test case is that the error is detected but the program does not break out of the loop or prevent further tokenization, though the spec says each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error and output should go to error.txt. The real bug is that after setting ErrorLine, the lexer continues parsing instead of terminating early, but more importantly, it doesn't verify that pos+1 is valid before accessing sourceCode.charAt(pos + 1), leading to runtime exception on trailing '&' or '|'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer records the error line but does not stop processing or skip the rest of the input properly. More critically, it fails to check if pos+1 is within bounds, which can cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' appears at the end of file. Additionally, after detecting the error, it only increments pos by 1 and continues, potentially causing further misparsing or missing the actual error location if newline hasn't been accounted for. However, the primary issue relevant to the test case is that the error is detected but the program does not break out of the loop or prevent further tokenization, though the spec says each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error and output should go to error.txt. The real bug is that after setting ErrorLine, the lexer continues parsing instead of terminating early, but more importantly, it doesn't verify that pos+1 is valid before accessing sourceCode.charAt(pos + 1), leading to runtime exception on trailing '&' or '|'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer records the error but continues scanning; however, it fails to skip the erroneous character properly in all cases and does not break out of the loop, potentially causing repeated errors or incorrect state. More critically, it does not ensure that pos + 1 is within bounds before accessing sourceCode.charAt(pos + 1), which can cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' appears at the end of the file. Additionally, after detecting the error, it only increments pos by 1 but does not consume the rest of the invalid token safely, though the main issue here is missing bounds check.]
